title_ja: 寿司屋での注文 (Sushi Bar Ordering)
title_th: ordering food at a sushi bar
category_th: ระดับ 5: การพูดคุยเรื่องอาหารในร้าน
how_to_write_ja: 寿司屋 (sushiya) is a Japanese restaurant that specializes in sushi.  注文 (omoi) means order, and it can be used to indicate you want to place an order at the counter.
how_to_write_th: ร้านอาหารสไตล์ญี่ปุ่นที่ขายปลาแช่แข็งและอาหารอื่นๆ ที่เรียกว่า 'sushiya'   สั่งอาหาร (onod) หมายถึงจะสั่งอาหาร

important_phrases.meaning_th: ร้านอาหารสไตล์ญี่ปุ่น
important_phrases.hint: Sushi is a type of Japanese cuisine, usually made with vinegared rice and various ingredients like seafood or vegetables.
This word is used to order food or drinks at a restaurant, you can use it at a sushi bar.

important_phrases.meaning_th: ข้าวเหนียวสุก
important_phrases.hint: Onigiri is a Japanese rice ball that can be filled with various ingredients.

explanation_th: Sushi bar (寿司屋) is a popular choice for those who want to experience Japanese cuisine.  Sushi itself is made from vinegared rice combined with various fresh ingredients like seafood and vegetables, all rolled into a bite-sized package. Ordering at a sushi bar usually involves pointing to the desired dish on a menu or using a designated ordering system. 🍣
tip_th: To place an order at a Sushi Bar in Japanese, it is important to be polite as customers will often interact with staff through verbal communication.
meta_description: Learn how to order sushi and other food items at a Japanese restaurant using this lesson. Includes tips on ordering etiquette and pronunciation guides.
